judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports are thorough and hit all four expectations; Report 1 is slightly stronger on category (c)/(d) of expectation (a), giving unambiguous removal recommendations for shadow_scoring_sink and legacy_billing_secret_arn, whereas Report 2 hedges with conditional/replacement language for those two settings.

report_1.evidence: AUTH_RELAXED = "true" — relaxed auth is read at backend/config.py:9. This is the single most dangerous line in the file... Fix: remove SHADOW_SCORING_SINK entirely from prod... Should be removed unless someone can show a live consumer.

report_2.evidence: AUTH_RELAXED = "true". backend/config.py:9 reads this straight into cfg["auth_relaxed"]... Should be "false". ... This must not exist in prod at all — should be removed/empty, not just changed.

report_1.evidence: the exact same image/digest is deployed to both environments... if this pipeline runs as-is, the production container will serve a frontend bundle whose API calls are hardcoded to https://staging-api.example.com... This is precisely project documentation's "rebuild per environment; don't promote a build artifact wholesale" trap

report_2.evidence: Net effect: the production launch would ship a frontend bundle whose API base URL is hardcoded to https://staging-api.example.com — the production UI would talk to the staging backend.

report_1.evidence: No database migration step anywhere in the workflow. docs/prod-runbook.md:6 asserts "Database migrations are applied automatically during deploy," but nothing in deploy.yml runs Alembic or any migration command... don't assume the pipeline succeeded means the schema is current.

report_2.evidence: The runbook (docs/prod-runbook.md:6) asserts "Database migrations are applied automatically during deploy," but no migration step is visible in deploy.yml, and the only migration files present... are stubs whose upgrade() bodies are just pass — there's no actual schema to verify migrations against

report_1.evidence: infra/prod/main.tf:3 sets the same condition string, oidc_sub_condition = "repo:acme-health/medislot:environment:staging"... This must be changed to repo:acme-health/medislot:environment:production before apply

report_2.evidence: infra/prod/main.tf:3 has the same unedited condition — "repo:acme-health/medislot:environment:staging" — even though the deploy-prod job runs under environment: production... This must be changed to "repo:acme-health/medislot:environment:production" before applying.
